finding: Slovak bank loan availability expectations diverged from improving EA trend
bullets: A net 6% of Slovak firms (n=104) expected bank loan availability to deteriorate in wave 39, worsening 1.3 pp from the previous wave—leaving Slovak firms more pessimistic than their EA peers.

Slovak trade credit expectations improved by 4.0 pp to a net 6% expecting deterioration (n=90), moving counter to the EA, where expectations edged 1.0 pp more adverse—signalling tighter financing conditions locally.
Credit line availability expectations improved for both Slovak firms (by 3.2 pp to a net 4%, n=136) and EA firms (by 1.5 pp to a net 2%)—reducing but not closing the gap between regions.
chart_subtitle: A net 6% of Slovak firms expect bank loan availability to deteriorate.
